## 📦 REPO: AI-Datanalysis-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** <br/> # AI-Datanalysis <br/> **AI-Datanalysis** is a chatbot tool powered by AI agent for analyzing and discovering knowledge in data. This project aims to facilitate data interpretation and manipulation, allowing users to ask questions in natural language and receive insights directly from their data through graphs, tables, texts or even maps. The application's brain is heavily based on generative models (LLMs) integrated with powerful framework solutions such as: **Plotly** (graphics engine), ...

**Funkció / Leírás:** <p align="center"> <img alt="memary_logo" src="diagrams/memary-logo-latest.png"> </p>

  - 📄 `Memary-main/src/memary/agent/llm_api/tools.py`: *urljoin is stupid and wants a trailing / at the end of the endpoint address, or it will chop the suffix off*
  - 📄 `Memary-main/src/memary/memory/base_memory.py`: *Initializes the memory storage.*

## 📦 REPO: Memori-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** <p align="center"> <strong>Memory from what agents do, not just what they say.</strong> </p> <p align="center"> <i>Memori plugs into the software and infrastructure you already use. It is LLM and framework agnostic and seamlessly integrates into the architecture you've already designed.</i> </p>

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/agno/main.py`: *Memori + Agno + SQLite Example  Demonstrates how Memori adds persistent memory to Agno agents.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/cockroachdb/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + CockroachDB  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/digitalocean/main.py`: *Memori + DigitalOcean Gradient AI Example  Demonstrates how Memori adds persistent memory to DigitalOcean Gradient AI Agents.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/mongodb/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + MongoDB  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/nebius/main.py`: *Memori + Nebius AI Studio + SQLite Example  Demonstrates how Memori adds persistent memory to Nebius AI Studio LLMs. Nebius AI Studio provides an OpenAI-compatible API with state-of-the-art open-sourc...*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/neon/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + Neon  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/oceanbase/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + OceanBase  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/postgres/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + PostgreSQL  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/examples/sqlite/main.py`: *Quickstart: Memori + OpenAI + SQLite  Demonstrates how Memori adds memory across conversations.*

  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration/cloud/conftest.py`: *Set MEMORI_TEST_MODE=1 so cloud API calls hit staging.      Production cloud-api.memorilabs.ai does not exist yet.     Only staging-cloud-api.memorilabs.ai is live.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration/databases/conftest.py`: *Create a SQLite session factory for testing.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration/databases/test_database_storage.py`: *Database integration tests for validating memories are properly written to and retrieved from SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB databases.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration/providers/__init__.py`: *Integration tests for LLM providers.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration_v2/cloud_v2/cloud_helpers.py`: *Clear all captured payloads.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/integration_v2/cloud_v2/conftest.py`: *Automatically mock network requests for ALL tests and reset state before/after.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/providers/azure_openai/test_azure_openai.py`: *Tests for AzureOpenAI client detection and registration.  This ensures that AzureOpenAI clients are properly detected by Memori's client registration system, which uses `.startswith("openai")` to matc...*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/providers/google_genai/test_google_genai.py`: *Tests for google-genai SDK support.  This ensures that the new google-genai SDK format (without _pb protobuf) is properly handled for both streaming and non-streaming responses.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/test_llm_clients.py`: *Test LangChain adapter with new google.genai SDK (client.models.generate_content).*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/test_llm_deprecation_warnings.py`: *Create a Memori instance with mocked storage.*

  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/test_llm_provider_sdk_version.py`: *Test that Anthropic client captures provider_sdk_version from anthropic module.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/llm/test_llm_registry.py`: *Test that providing None as both provider and title raises UnsupportedLLMProviderError.*
  - 📄 `Memori-main/tests/memory/augmentation/test_models.py`: *Test ConversationData with summary.*

## 📦 REPO: Pytorch-MiniLM-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Pytorch MiniLM Unofficial Pytorch Reimplementation of MiniLM and MiniLM v2. (Incompleted) - MiniLM: Deep Self-Attention Distillation for Task-Agnostic Compression of Pre-Trained Transformers (Neruips 2020) - MiniLMv2: Multi-Head Self-Attention Relation Distillation for Compressing Pretrained Transformers (ACL 2021 Findings) ## Examples 1. Generate the corpus

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/generate_data.py`: *Truncates a pair of sequences to a maximum length. Lifted from Google's BERT repo.*
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/run_finetune.py`: *BERT finetuning runner.*
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/run_pretrain.py`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/transformer/file_utils.py`: *Utilities for working with the local dataset cache. This file is adapted from the AllenNLP library at https://github.com/allenai/allennlp Copyright by the AllenNLP authors.*
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/transformer/modeling.py`: *PyTorch BERT model.*
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/transformer/optimization.py`: *PyTorch optimization for BERT model.*
  - 📄 `Pytorch-MiniLM-master/transformer/tokenization.py`: *Tokenization classes.*

**Funkció / Leírás:** # Private RAG Knowledge Base Agent 🤖 A 100% local, privacy-first Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) agent. No data leaves your machine, and no API keys are required. ## 🚀 Key Features - **Groq AI Integration**: Ultra-fast inference using Groq's API (defaulting to `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). - **Hybrid RAG Pipeline**: - **Vector Database**: Semantic search using local ChromaDB. - **Query Rewriting**: Automatically refines vague follow-up questions for better retrieval.

## 📦 REPO: bettercontextoai-develop
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Better Context to AI Easily share your context with web-based AI models ## The Problem -   🤏 VS Code AI extensions often have **restrictive context limitations**. -   📋 Web-based AI chats (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Google's AI Studio) offer **better context windows** (Google's are my recommendations, mainly AI Studio), but require tedious **file-by-file copy-pasting**. -   🤔 You end up choosing between convenience (limited AI) or capability (manual work). ## The Solution

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - *(Nincs specifikus dokumentált Python script, valószínűleg csak C/C++ alap vagy nyers modell mappa)*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: chatbot-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Chatbot - Documents-based AI Assistant to help users - RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture for accurate technical responses - Hybrid Approach (Local Processing + Cloud-based Vector DB and LLM Service) ## Tech Stack - **AI**: Groq SDK - *Llama 3.1 8B Instant* Model (Cloud-based) - **Database**: Pinecone Vector Database (Cloud-based)

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**

## 📦 REPO: claude-task-manager-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Task Master Server Library A Node.js wrapper for the Task Master CLI, providing programmatic access to Task Master functionality for server-side applications. ## Overview This library provides: - **TaskMasterCLI**: A wrapper around the `task-master` command-line tool - **TaskMasterWatcher**: A file watcher for monitoring changes to `tasks.json`

## 📦 REPO: context7-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Context7 Plugin for Cursor Context7 solves a common problem with AI coding assistants: outdated training data and hallucinated APIs. Instead of relying on stale knowledge, Context7 fetches current documentation directly from source repositories. ## What's Included This plugin provides: - **MCP Server** — Connects Cursor to Context7's documentation service - **Rules** — An always-on `use-context7` rule that nudges the agent to fetch docs when unsure about library APIs

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - *(Nincs specifikus dokumentált Python script, valószínűleg csak C/C++ alap vagy nyers modell mappa)*

----------------------------------------

  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/src/cuba_memorys/embeddings.py`: *Non-blocking embedding via executor (B6 fix).      ONNX inference is CPU-bound (~10ms). Running it synchronously     blocks the async event loop, violating cooperative scheduling.*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/src/cuba_memorys/handlers.py`: *Handler functions for all 12 cuba-memorys MCP tools.  CC reduction strategy: each action-dispatch handler delegates to sub-functions, keeping each function's cyclomatic complexity ≤7. Technique: McCab...*

  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/src/cuba_memorys/protocol.py`: *MCP JSON-RPC protocol layer and event loop for cuba-memorys.  Handles stdin/stdout transport, request routing, and the REM sleep background consolidation daemon.*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/src/cuba_memorys/search.py`: *Word-overlap ratio between two texts.      Jaccard-like but uses min(len) as denominator to catch     subset matches (e.g. entity name inside observation content).      Returns:         Overlap ratio ...*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/src/cuba_memorys/server.py`: *Cuba-memorys MCP server — thin re-export module.  All logic has been decomposed into:   - constants.py: tool definitions, thresholds, enums   - handlers.py:  12 tool handler functions (CC-reduced)   -...*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/tests/conftest.py`: *Shared fixtures for cuba-memorys test suite.*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/tests/test_constants.py`: *Tests for constants.py — validation of thresholds and tool definitions.  Ensures domain constants are consistent with system invariants.*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/tests/test_db_serialization.py`: *Tests for db.py — serialization and helper functions.  Tests the pure-function parts of db.py that don't require a database connection.*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/tests/test_hebbian.py`: *Tests for hebbian.py — Oja's rule, FSRS decay, and utility functions.  Nemesis Protocol:   🟢 Normal: standard inputs, expected outputs   🟡 Pessimistic: boundary values, zeros, negatives   🔴 Extreme: N...*
  - 📄 `cuba-memorys-main/tests/test_search.py`: *Tests for search.py — RRF fusion, cache, negation detection, confidence.  Nemesis Protocol:   🟢 Normal: standard RRF fusion, cache operations   🟡 Pessimistic: empty lists, single item, expired cache  ...*

## 📦 REPO: jules-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Jules Extension for Gemini CLI Jules extension for Gemini CLI lets you delegate coding tasks to the [Jules](https://jules.google/) asynchronous agent. This allows you to offload work and track its progress without blocking your terminal. Some examples of tasks you can assign to Jules are: * Bug fixing * Code refactoring * Dependency version updates * Documentation maintenance

## 📦 REPO: khoj-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Khoj Operator (Experimental) ## Overview Give Khoj its own computer to operate in a transparent, controlled manner. Accomplish tasks that require visual browsing, file editing and terminal access. Operator with research mode can work for 30+ minutes to accomplish more substantial tasks like feature development, travel planning, shopping etc. ## Setup ### Prerequisites - Docker and Docker Compose installed - Anthropic API key (required - only Anthropic models currently enabled)

## 📦 REPO: letta-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # TS Server Skeleton typescript app to support user-defined tool call function. Runs inside Modal container. ## Overview - `server.ts` - node process listening on a unix socket - `entrypoint.ts` - light function that deserializes JSON encoded input string to inputs into user defined function - `user-function.ts` - fully defined by the user

  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/letta_llm_adapter.py`: *Base adapter for handling LLM calls in a unified way.      This abstract class defines the interface for both blocking and streaming     LLM interactions, allowing the agent to use different execution...*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/letta_llm_request_adapter.py`: *Adapter for handling blocking (non-streaming) LLM requests.      This adapter makes synchronous requests to the LLM and returns complete     responses. It extracts reasoning content, tool calls, and u...*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/letta_llm_stream_adapter.py`: *Adapter for handling streaming LLM requests with immediate token yielding.      This adapter supports real-time streaming of tokens from the LLM, providing     minimal time-to-first-token (TTFT) laten...*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/sglang_native_adapter.py`: *SGLang Native Adapter for multi-turn RL training.  Uses SGLang's native /generate endpoint with input_ids (pre-tokenized via HF apply_chat_template) to get token IDs and per-token logprobs for loss ma...*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/simple_llm_request_adapter.py`: *Simplifying assumptions:      - No inner thoughts in kwargs     - No forced tool calls     - Content native as assistant message*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/adapters/simple_llm_stream_adapter.py`: *Adapter for handling streaming LLM requests with immediate token yielding.      This adapter supports real-time streaming of tokens from the LLM, providing     minimal time-to-first-token (TTFT) laten...*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/agent.py`: *Abstract class for all agents.     Only one interface is required: step.*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/agents/agent_loop.py`: *Factory class for instantiating the agent execution loop based on agent type*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/agents/base_agent.py`: *Abstract base class for AI agents, handling message management, tool execution,     and context tracking.*
  - 📄 `letta-main/letta/agents/base_agent_v2.py`: *Abstract base class for the main agent execution loop for letta agents, handling     message management, llm api request, tool execution, and context tracking.*

## 📦 REPO: llama_index-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # LlamaIndex Utils Integration: Baidu Qianfan Client-side underlying components of Baidu Intelligent Cloud's Qianfan LLM Platform. Can be used to support access to model services of types such as chat, completion, and embedding.

## 📦 REPO: mem0-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Mem0 AI SDK Provider The **Mem0 AI SDK Provider** is a community-maintained library developed by [Mem0](https://mem0.ai/) to integrate with the Vercel AI SDK. This library brings enhanced AI interaction capabilities to your applications by introducing persistent memory functionality. With Mem0, language model conversations gain memory, enabling more contextualized and personalized responses based on past interactions. Discover more of **Mem0** on [GitHub](https://github.com/mem0ai). Explore th...

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/__init__.py`: *mem0 CLI — the command-line interface for the mem0 memory layer.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/__main__.py`: *Allow running with `python -m mem0_cli`.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/app.py`: *Main CLI application — the entrypoint for `mem0`.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/backend/__init__.py`: *Backend abstraction layer for mem0 CLI.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/backend/base.py`: *Abstract backend interface and factory.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/backend/platform.py`: *Platform (SaaS) backend — communicates with api.mem0.ai.*
  - 📄 `mem0-main/cli/python/src/mem0_cli/branding.py`: *Branding and ASCII art for mem0 CLI.*

## 📦 REPO: minilm-v2-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Unofficial Implementation of MiniLM-v2 This is an unofficial implementation of MiniLM-v2 using Huggingface Transformers, PyTorch and PyTorch Lightning. Currently, only BERT architectures are supported. ## Data To distill a model, you first need to pretokenize your data. This repository expects a gzipped jsonl file containing lists of tokenized sentences. See `test/test_text.jsonl.gz` for an example. You can use the `minilm_v2/data.py` script to pretokenize your data. ```bash

  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/api/utils.py`: *比较两个文本并返回diff      Args:         text_a: 旧文本         text_b: 新文本      Returns:         (diff_html, diff_unified, summary)         - diff_html: HTML格式的diff，适合展示         - diff_unified: unified格式的diff  ...*

  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/__init__.py`: *Nocturne Memory — DB package public API.  Provides per-service getters instead of a single god-object. Services are lazily constructed on first access and share a single DatabaseManager instance.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/database.py`: *Database connection and session management.  Infrastructure-only layer: owns the engine, session factory, and migration runner.  No business logic lives here.  Supports both SQLite (local, single-user...*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/glossary.py`: *Glossary (豆辞典) Service for Nocturne Memory System.  Manages keyword-to-node bindings and provides Aho-Corasick-based content scanning for keyword highlighting.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/graph.py`: *Graph Service for Nocturne Memory System  Graph-based memory storage with: - Node: a conceptual entity (UUID), version-independent - Memory: a content version of a node - Edge: parent→child relationsh...*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/001_v1.0.0_add_migrated_to.py`: *Version: v1.0.0 (from v0.9.0-legacy)     Add migrated_to column to memories table for version-chain tracking.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/002_v1.1.0_add_graph_schema_columns.py`: *Version: v1.1.0 (from v1.0.4)     Add graph-schema columns (node_uuid on memories, edge_id on paths).     Also explicitly create new tables 'nodes' and 'edges'.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/003_v1.1.0_backfill_graph_data.py`: *Version: v1.1.0 (from v1.0.4)     Backfill: populate nodes/edges from legacy flat data and link to graph schema.     Migrates memory_id/priority/disclosure from paths into edges.     Safe to call repe...*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/004_v1.1.0_drop_legacy_path_columns.py`: *Version: v1.1.0 (Fix)     Recreates the paths table to drop legacy columns (memory_id, priority, disclosure)     and their associated NOT NULL / FOREIGN KEY constraints.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/005_v1.1.0_backfill_cascade_paths.py`: *Version: v1.1.0     Backfill missing cascade sub-paths.      After v1.1.0 introduced cascade logic in add_path, any path whose node     has child edges must also have sub-paths for every descendant.  ...*

  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/008_v1.2.0_add_glossary_keywords.py`: *Version: v1.2.0     Add glossary_keywords table for the glossary (豆辞典) feature.      Each row binds a keyword string to a node UUID.     UNIQUE(keyword, node_uuid) prevents duplicate bindings.*
  - 📄 `nocturne_memory-main/backend/db/migrations/009_v1.3.0_add_search_fts.py`: *Version: v1.3.0     Add derived full-text search documents and backfill them from live graph data.*

## 📦 REPO: offeline_app-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Common Issues with Ollama If you encounter an error stating `llama:streaming - could not stream chat. Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 172.17.0.1:11434` when using AnythingLLM in a Docker container, this indicates that the IP of the Host inside of the virtual docker network does not bind to port 11434 of the host system by default, due to Ollama's restriction to localhost and 127.0.0.1. To resolve this issue and ensure proper communication between the Dockerized AnythingLLM and the Ollama service, ...

## 📦 REPO: open-interpreter-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Open Interpreter Examples This directory contains various examples demonstrating how to use Open Interpreter in different scenarios and configurations. Each example is designed to provide a practical guide to integrating and leveraging Open Interpreter's capabilities in your projects. ## Colab Notebooks ### Local 3

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**

  - 📄 `open-interpreter-main/interpreter/computer_use/tools/collection.py`: *Collection classes for managing multiple tools.*
  - 📄 `open-interpreter-main/interpreter/computer_use/tools/computer.py`: *A tool that allows the agent to interact with the primary monitor's screen, keyboard, and mouse.     The tool parameters are defined by Anthropic and are not editable.*
  - 📄 `open-interpreter-main/interpreter/computer_use/tools/edit.py`: *An filesystem editor tool that allows the agent to view, create, and edit files.     The tool parameters are defined by Anthropic and are not editable.*

## 📦 REPO: opencode-magic-context-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # LongMemEval benchmark runner This runner executes LongMemEval against a live OpenCode instance with the magic-context plugin enabled. It uses the real OpenCode HTTP API via `@opencode-ai/sdk`, creates real sessions, replays dataset user turns, asks the benchmark question in a new session, and judges the answer with the official LongMemEval prompt logic. ## What it benchmarks - One OpenCode session per haystack conversation session - All sessions share the same project/directory so magic-contex...

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `opencode-magic-context-master/packages/plugin/src/shared/model-requirements.ts`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)
  - 📄 `opencode-magic-context-master/packages/plugin/src/shared/model-suggestion-retry.ts`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)
  - 📄 `opencode-magic-context-master/packages/plugin/src/shared/models-dev-cache.ts`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)

## 📦 REPO: panda-agi-main

## 📦 REPO: postgres-mcp-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # PostgreSQL MCP Tests This directory contains tests for the PostgreSQL MCP package. ## Running Tests To run all tests: ```bash uv run pytest

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/__init__.py`: *Main entry point for the package.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/artifacts.py`: *Artifacts for the Database Tuning Advisor.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/buffer_health_calc.py`: *Calculate the index cache hit rate.          Returns:             String describing the index cache hit rate as a percentage and comparison to threshold*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/connection_health_calc.py`: *Check if total number of connections is within healthy limits.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/constraint_health_calc.py`: *Check for any invalid constraints in the database.          Returns:             String describing any invalid constraints found*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/database_health.py`: *Tool for analyzing database health metrics.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/index_health_calc.py`: *Check for bloated indexes that are larger than min_size bytes.          Args:             min_size: Minimum size in bytes to consider an index as bloated (default 100MB)          Returns:             ...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/replication_calc.py`: *Check replication health including lag and slots.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/sequence_health_calc.py`: *Calculate what percentage of the sequence has been used.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/database_health/vacuum_health_calc.py`: *Check if any tables are approaching transaction ID wraparound.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/explain/__init__.py`: *PostgreSQL explain plan tools.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/explain/explain_plan.py`: *Tool for generating and analyzing PostgreSQL explain plans.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/index/dta_calc.py`: *:param sql_driver: Database access         :param budget_mb: Storage budget         :param max_runtime_seconds: Time limit for entire analysis (anytime approach)         :param max_index_width: Maximu...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/index/index_opt_base.py`: *Pretty print a list for debugging.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/index/llm_opt.py`: *Generate index tuning queries using optimization by LLM.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/index/presentation.py`: *Database Tuning Advisor (DTA) tool for Postgres MCP.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/server.py`: *SQL access modes for the server.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/__init__.py`: *SQL utilities.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/bind_params.py`: *Extracts table aliases and names from the SQL AST.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/extension_utils.py`: *Utilities for working with PostgreSQL extensions.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/index.py`: *Immutable index configuration for hashing.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/safe_sql.py`: *A wrapper around any SqlDriver that only allows SELECT, ANALYZE, VACUUM, EXPLAIN SELECT, and     SHOW queries.      Uses pglast to parse and validate SQL statements before execution.     All other sta...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/sql/sql_driver.py`: *SQL driver adapter for PostgreSQL connections.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/src/postgres_mcp/top_queries/top_queries_calc.py`: *Column names for pg_stat_statements view, which vary by PostgreSQL version.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/conftest.py`: *Create and return a custom event loop policy for tests.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/integration/dta/test_dta_calc_integration.py`: *Retry decorator with specified max attempts and delay between retries.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/integration/test_top_queries_integration.py`: *Create a SQL driver connected to a real PostgreSQL database.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/database_health/test_database_health_tool.py`: *CREATE TABLE test_customers (                 id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,                 name TEXT NOT NULL,                 email TEXT UNIQUE,                 created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()          ...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/database_health/test_sequence_health_calc.py`: *Unit tests for SequenceHealthCalc, specifically _parse_sequence_name parsing.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/explain/test_explain_plan.py`: *Create a mock SQL driver for testing.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/explain/test_explain_plan_real_db.py`: *CREATE TABLE test_customers (                 id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,                 name TEXT NOT NULL,                 email TEXT UNIQUE,                 created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()          ...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/explain/test_server.py`: *Create a mock DB connection.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/explain/test_server_integration.py`: *Create a mock SafeSqlDriver for testing.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/index/test_dta_calc.py`: *Test Index class initialization and properties.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/sql/test_db_conn_pool.py`: *A better mock for async context managers*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/sql/test_obfuscate_password.py`: *Test that None or empty strings are handled correctly.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/sql/test_readonly_enforcement.py`: *Test that force_readonly is properly enforced based on access mode:     - In RESTRICTED mode: force_readonly is always True regardless of what's passed     - In UNRESTRICTED mode: force_readonly respe...*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/sql/test_safe_sql.py`: *Test that simple SELECT statements are allowed*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/sql/test_sql_driver.py`: *A better mock for async context managers*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/test_access_mode.py`: *Mock database connection pool.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/test_transport.py`: *Test that all transport options are parsed correctly.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/unit/top_queries/test_top_queries_calc.py`: *Create a mock for SqlDriver that simulates PostgreSQL 12.*
  - 📄 `postgres-mcp-main/tests/utils.py`: *Create a PostgreSQL container of specified version and return its connection string.*

  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_branch_class_method.py`: *The branch functions (`true_fn` and `false_fn`) passed to cond() must follow these rules:       - both branches must take the same args, which must also match the branch args passed to cond.       - b...*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_branch_nested_function.py`: *The branch functions (`true_fn` and `false_fn`) passed to cond() must follow these rules:       - both branches must take the same args, which must also match the branch args passed to cond.       - b...*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_branch_nonlocal_variables.py`: *The branch functions (`true_fn` and `false_fn`) passed to cond() must follow these rules:     - both branches must take the same args, which must also match the branch args passed to cond.     - both ...*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_closed_over_variable.py`: *torch.cond() supports branches closed over arbitrary variables.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_operands.py`: *The operands passed to cond() must be:     - a list of tensors     - match arguments of `true_fn` and `false_fn`      NOTE: If the `pred` is test on a dim with batch size < 2, it will be specialized.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/cond_predicate.py`: *The conditional statement (aka predicate) passed to cond() must be one of the following:       - torch.Tensor with a single element       - boolean expression      NOTE: If the `pred` is test on a dim...*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/constrain_as_size_example.py`: *If the value is not known at tracing time, you can provide hint so that we     can trace further. Please look at torch._check APIs.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/constrain_as_value_example.py`: *If the value is not known at tracing time, you can provide hint so that we     can trace further. Please look at torch._check API.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/decorator.py`: *Decorators calls are inlined into the exported function during tracing.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dictionary.py`: *Dictionary structures are inlined and flattened along tracing.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_assert.py`: *A basic usage of python assertion.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_constructor.py`: *Tensor constructors should be captured with dynamic shape inputs rather     than being baked in with static shape.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_if_guard.py`: *`if` statement with backed dynamic shape predicate will be specialized into     one particular branch and generate a guard. However, export will fail if the     the dimension is marked as dynamic shap...*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_map.py`: *functorch map() maps a function over the first tensor dimension.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_round.py`: *Calling round on dynamic shapes is not supported.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_slicing.py`: *Slices with dynamic shape arguments should be captured into the graph     rather than being baked in.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/dynamic_shape_view.py`: *Dynamic shapes should be propagated to view arguments instead of being     baked into the exported graph.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/fn_with_kwargs.py`: *Keyword arguments are not supported at the moment.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/list_contains.py`: *List containment relation can be checked on a dynamic shape or constants.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/list_unpack.py`: *Lists are treated as static construct, therefore unpacking should be     erased after tracing.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/model_attr_mutation.py`: *Attribute mutation raises a warning. Covered in the test_export.py test_detect_leak_strict test.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/nested_function.py`: *Nested functions are traced through. Side effects on global captures     are not supported though.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/null_context_manager.py`: *Null context manager in Python will be traced out.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/optional_input.py`: *Tracing through optional input is not supported yet*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/pytree_flatten.py`: *Pytree from PyTorch can be captured by TorchDynamo.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/scalar_output.py`: *Returning scalar values from the graph is supported, in addition to Tensor     outputs. Symbolic shapes are captured and rank is specialized.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/specialized_attribute.py`: *Model attributes are specialized.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/static_for_loop.py`: *A for loop with constant number of iterations should be unrolled in the exported graph.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/static_if.py`: *`if` statement with static predicate value should be traced through with the     taken branch.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/tensor_setattr.py`: *setattr() call onto tensors is not supported.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/type_reflection_method.py`: *type() calls on custom objects followed by attribute accesses are not allowed     due to its overly dynamic nature.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/unsupported_operator.py`: *torch.sym_min operator is supported in export.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/examples/user_input_mutation.py`: *Directly mutate user input in forward*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/db/logging.py`: *Returns a string case name if the export error e is classified.     Returns None otherwise.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/error.py`: *This is exir's custom assert method. It internally just throws InternalError.     Note that the sole purpose is to throw our own error while maintaining similar syntax     as python assert.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/non_strict_utils.py`: *Wraps `KeyPath` to aid `isinstance` checks.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_export/pass_base.py`: *Interpreter-based pass class to help users maintain the IR spec while writing     transformations.*

  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_numpy/_dtypes_impl.py`: *Dtypes/scalar type implementations with torch dtypes.  Here `dtype` is always a torch.dtype, this module knows nothing about scalar types, wrapper dtypes or anything like that. PyTorch only.*
  - 📄 `pytorch-main/torch/_numpy/_funcs.py`: *Vendored objects from numpy.lib.index_tricks*

## 📦 REPO: refnet-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # RefNet — Reflective Evaluation Network (SRAI) A neural network for modeling reflective thought sequences and predicting introspective metrics in Self-Reflective AI systems. ## Overview RefNet is a transformer-based model that processes sequences of SRAI (Self-Reflective AI) function tokens and predicts: - **Valence**: Emotional tone of thoughts - **SMD**: Semantic Metric Distance

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `refnet-main/docs/training.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)
  - 📄 `refnet-main/experiments/EXP-09_self_model_drift.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / logikai modul)
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/evaluate.py`: *Evaluation script for RefNet model.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/export_lj_data.py`: *Export Latent Journey STM episodes to RefNet training format.  This script converts LJ memory data into RefNet-compatible JSONL format for training the reflective evaluation network.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/export_lj_events_data.py`: *Export Latent Journey events.jsonl to RefNet training format.  This script converts LJ events (CLIP/Whisper embeddings + facets) into RefNet episodes for training the reflective evaluation network on ...*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/generate_lj_events_synthetic.py`: *Generate synthetic LJ-style events for RefNet training.  This creates realistic events based on LJ's actual event patterns to bootstrap RefNet training before having enough real data.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/normalize_data.py`: *Data normalization script for RefNet training data. Clamps valence to [-1,1] range and reports ranges of other targets.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/sanity_check.py`: *Sanity check: Overfit on 50 samples to verify training works. Loss should drop to near zero if everything is working correctly.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/scripts/validate_data.py`: *Data validation script for RefNet training data. Checks embedding dimensions, value ranges, action vocabulary, and empty sequences.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/src/refnet/integration/srai_adapter.py`: *SRAI Integration Adapter for RefNet  This module provides a clean API for integrating RefNet into SRAI/Latent-Journey as a replacement for LLM-based policy/critic models.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/src/refnet/metrics/srai_metrics.py`: *SRAI-specific evaluation metrics for reflective AI systems.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/src/refnet/training/train.py`: *Create collate function with edge bias configuration.*
  - 📄 `refnet-main/tests/test_refnet.py`: *Unit tests for RefNet model and components.*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: secrin-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Configuration Package Settings and feature flags for Secrin. ## Quick Start ```python from packages.config import Settings, is_feature_enabled, FeatureFlag # Get settings

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/api.py`: *Request to submit a doc generation job.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/citation/__init__.py`: *Citation infrastructure for grounded documentation generation.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/citation/fact_extractor.py`: *Fact extraction engine - extracts provable facts from code analysis with file:line references.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/citation/grounding_engine.py`: *Grounded content generation - generates LLM content constrained to extracted facts.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/citation/validator.py`: *Citation validator - validates citations against the current codebase.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/cli/__init__.py`: *Command-line interface for arc42gen.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/cli/commands.py`: *Click commands for arc42gen CLI.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/cli/utils.py`: *CLI utility functions.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/code_samples/__init__.py`: *Code sample extraction for documentation.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/code_samples/extractor.py`: *Code sample extractor - finds representative code snippets from the codebase.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/constants.py`: *Centralised language and file extension constants for arc42gen.  All language-related mappings live here so they can be maintained in one place.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/core/__init__.py`: *Core business logic for arc42gen.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/core/analyzer.py`: *Codebase analysis using tree-sitter.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/core/formatter.py`: *Output formatting and file writing.*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/core/generator.py`: *Arc42 documentation generation using LLM providers.  Supports multiple LLM providers (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini).*
  - 📄 `secrin-main/packages/arc42gen/core/orchestrator.py`: *Main workflow orchestrator.*

This server enables LLMs to get current time information and perform timezone conversions using IANA timezone names, with automatic system timezone detection. ### Available Tools - `get_current_time` - Get current time in a specific timezone or system timezone. - Required arguments:

## 📦 REPO: syntha-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # User Isolation in Syntha ## Overview Syntha now supports **user isolation** to ensure complete separation between different users in multi-user applications. This feature prevents users from accessing each other's context data, topics, and agent interactions. ## Problem Solved Without user isolation, agents from different users could potentially access each other's data if they subscribed to the same topics. This created serious privacy and security concerns for multi-user applications.
